task:
[Instruction] You are given an excerpt from a script: 
 (opening:     The camera slowly zooms through a variety of insects and rocks. We follow a small yellow bug climb up a blade of grass.  As it spreads its wings to fly  we are whisked along the Australian outback and prairie by Ayers rock and eventually slow down as we approach Cody's house.)

(scene:  inside Cody's room.  The camera pans around to show Cody sleeping in his hammock.  The sound of Faloo's call is heard.  Cody hears it  jumps out of bed  and runs to the window.  He puts on his shirt and grabs his knife.)

(scene:  Cody sneaks past his mother who is in the kitchen listening to the radio.)

Announcer:    ... thundershowers are expected in the Crocodile Falls area and some of the surrounding gullies so take out your...

(scene:  Outside Cody's house.  Cody leaves the house  and closes the door behind him  but not quietly.)

Mom:     (from inside upon hearing the door) Cody!

Cody:    (whincing) Yeah mom?

Mom:     What about your breakfast?

Cody:    I've got some sandwiches in my pack.

Mom:     Well be home for supper.

Cody:    (hopping the gate) No worries mom.

(scene:  Cody runs toward the forest; Faloo's call is heard in the background.  He runs past some rock formations and enters the woods.  Birds follow him; and squak at him.)

Cody:    (to the birds) I know  I'm coming.
 (Cody jumps over a hollow log) Hustle up Nelson  Faloo's sounding the call!
 (Cody slides through a log  picks up a stick  and beats on the roof of the wombats home.) C'mon little wombats  hurry!
 (Cody continues to run through the forest with all of the animals following him.)
 (Cody arrives at the tree where Faloo has been sounding the call.)
 (to Faloo) Who's caught this time?

Faloo:   You don't know her  Cody  her name is Marahute  the great golden eagle.

Cody:    Where is she?

Faloo:   She's caught  high on a cliff in a poacher's trap.  You're the only one who can reach her.

Cody:    I'll get her loose.

Faloo:   Right-oh  hop on  no time to lose.
 (Cody hops onto Faloo and they travel through the forest and along a stream/river; more scenes of animals and the forest.)
 (They arrive at the cliff.)
 (pointing up towards the cliff) She's up on top of that ridge.  Be careful lit'l friend.

(scene:  various "time lapse" views of Cody climbing up the cliff.)
 (Cody reaches the top and sees the eagle.)

Cody:    Marahute!
 (Cody looks at the eagle; he approaches her slowly; she hears him and wakes up; Marahute screeches and struggles to get free.)
 (reassuring) Calm down  calm down.  I'm not gonna hurt you.  (Cody strokes Marahute on the head) That's a girl. Stay still... it's o.k.
 (Cody gets out his knife; Marahute sees the glint of the knife and    begins to struggle and scream)
 No wait!  I'm here to help you... easy!... easy!
 (Cody cuts two ropes.  Cody cuts the last rope to free Marahute.)
 You're free!!
 (As Marahute spreads her wings to fly  she knocks Cody off the cliff.)
 Aaaiigh!
 (Cody falls; Marahute dives down to catch him; she catches him just before he hits the ground; they begin to fly around; the animals see Cody on Marahute and stand in awe; Marahute files over several rock formations; the fly up above the clouds; Cody looks at his reflection in Marahute's eye.)
 Higher!
 (They fly even higher above the clouds; Marahute throws Cody and catches him; Cody is now held in Marahute's talons.)
 Woah!
 (Cody mocks an eagle screech; he laughs as Marahute tickles him; they cruise above the clouds which eventually open up to show the ground; Marahute nose dives towards the ground and a stream; she holds Cody just high enough above the water so that he is water skiing; they approach a flock of birds; Marahute lets Cody go and he skims through the birds  scattering them; Marahute grabs Cody just before he falls in and then put Cody right in front of her  on her beak (pushing him from behind); they go over the egde of a waterfall; Marahute catches Cody again; this time he rides by standing on her back; they arrive at Marahute's nest)
 Wow!
 (Cody and Marahute look at each other; Cody falls over as he attempts to look at Marahute upside down. Marahute moves some grass and feathers to show Cody her eggs)
 You're a mom!
 (Cody puts his ear to the eggs)
 They're very warm.  Are they gonna hatch soon?
 (Marahute ruffles her neck feathers in an affectionate manner; she sits on the eggs and then looks out "over her domain".)
 Where's the daddy eagle?  (Marahute drops her head) Oh... my dad's gone too.
 (Cody give Marahute an affectionate stroke;  as they fix the covering on the eggs  the wind picks up and blows a feather in Cody's face; he looks at it  plays with it  and puts it back.  Marahute picks it up and gives it to Cody and he gives her a hug.)
 (Marahute and Cody are now on the ground; Marahute takes off and Cody runs around making flying noises)

(scene:  just inside the forest.  A wanted poster of McLeach is posted on a tree; A mouse is tied up with a bell attached to it that rings as it struggles; Cody hears the bell and goes over to the mouse.)

Cody:    Heh heh... hey little fella  what happened to you?

Baitmouse:    (panicking) Oh no! No  no  no  no!!  Get away  get away! It's a trap  it's a trap.  Be careful  NO!

Cody:    (as the mouse is speaking) Don't worry  I'll get you loose.  Woah! (Cody falls into the trap.  He looks up to see a blinking light. 

[Requirement] Based on this, continue the scene and develop the storyline. The continuation must be {word_count_type} {word_count} words long.

response:
**INT. DEEP FOREST – DUSK**  
The forest hum is a low, resonant thrum, the evening sky trickling violet through the canopy. A faint, frantic *tink‑tink‑tink* of a tiny bell pulses from the tangled vines.

**CODY**  
*(kneeling, eyes wide, voice soft but urgent)*  
Hey, little buddy—hold on!  

He reaches into the thicket and pulls the **Baitmouse** free. The mouse’s fur bristles, its whiskers trembling as the bell hangs limp from its collar.

**Baitmouse**  
*(gasping, eyes darting)*  
It… it’s a snare! A *snap* trap—*wired* to a pressure plate. I was… I was trying to steal a crumb from a—  

A blur of darkness darts past the trees. The **pressure plate** beneath the mouse clicks ominously.

**CODY**  
*(gripping the mouse’s paw)*  
Don’t move. I’ll… I’ll disarm it.

Cody kneels, eyes scanning the crude metal contraption. He pulls a pocketknife, the blade catching a glint of the dying light.

**CODY (CONT'D)**  
*(whispering)*  
Come on, old friend, you and I have untied worse knots.

He nudges the plate gently; the trap’s spring releases with a *click* and a faint *ping* of metal hitting stone. The mouse squeaks, its tail flicking in relief.

**Baitmouse**  
*(breathing faster)*  
Thank you! Thank you! I— I was trying to get to the **map**. It’s tucked inside the hollow like a secret. The poachers— they left a trail!

A **FLUTTER** cuts through the trees. Faloo, the bright‑feathered bird, swoops down, perching on a low branch, eyes glinting with curiosity.

**FALOO**  
*(high‑pitched, rapid chirp)*  
Cody! The forest sings of danger— the poacher’s work is spreading like a shadow.  

**CODY**  
*(standing, cradling the mouse)*  
Faloo, what did they leave?  

Faloo flutters its wings, scattering tiny leaves that form a *glimmering outline* in the air: a **HAND‑DRAWN MAP** etched in charcoal, arrows pointing toward a **cave** with a jagged mouth, the symbol of an **eagle** perched beside a **crossed‑sword**.

**FALOO**  
*(chirps, then a deeper tone)*  
The map leads to **McLeach’s lair**, deep in the **Gnarled Gorge**. He’s the one who set the snare. He also—  

Faloo flaps abruptly, perched now on Cody’s shoulder, its beak nudging the map as if urging him forward.

**CODY**  
*(determined)*  
Alright, buddy. Time to end this.  

**CUT TO:**  

**EXT. GNAWLED GORGE – NIGHT**  
A moonlit chasm yawns, its walls black as basalt, echoing with the distant rush of subterranean rivers. The narrow path is strewn with **spikes** and **rusted cages**, remnants of failed hunts.  

Cody, armed with his knife, a **hand‑woven rope**, and the rescued Baitmouse tucked safely in his pocket, moves stealthily. Faloo flits ahead, a soft beacon of iridescent light.

**OVERHEAD SHOT** – The **WANTED POSTER** of **MCL E A CH** fluttering on a jagged outcrop: a scarred face, a dark cloak, a pistol tucked into his belt.

Cody's breath is barely audible as the wind whistles through the gorge, carrying an **ominous rustle**. The sound of **chains** clanking against rock grows louder.

**CODY**  
*(whispers)*  
Something’s up ahead.  

A **SHADOW** slides against the stone—a massive **cage**, its bars darkened by grime. Inside, a **GOLDEN EAGLE**—the **Great Marahute**—lies cowering, feathers ruffled, eyes reflecting fear. The cage is sealed with a **rusted lock** and a **tangled web of rope**.

Cody’s heart **pounds**; his mind flashes back to the earlier rescue.

**CODY (V.O.)**  
*(soft, echoing)*  
Marahute… I owe you.

He kneels, examining the lock. The **rusted latch** has a simple **keyhole**, but a thick **metal bar** blocks the rotation. Below the cage, a **small alcove** houses a **crude pulley system**—the poacher’s makeshift mechanism.

Faloo lands on the cage’s top, its beak pecking at a loose rope. The bird’s **sharp beak** silently cuts the rope, causing the pulley to **creak**.

**CODY**  
*(quick, urgent)*  
Hold steady, Marahute. We’ve got this.

Cody pulls the knife, **prying** at the bar. A **metallic groan** resonates through the gorge. The bar **shifts**, revealing the **keyhole**. He slides his knife in, turning with a **metallic screech** until the lock **clicks**.

The cage door **swing** opens a sliver, the golden light catching Cody’s face. Marahute’s majestic head lifts, her eyes burning with luminous amber.

**MARAHUTE**  
*(low, trembling)*  
Child… you…  

She attempts a **weak flap**, shaking the cage. Cody steps forward, **extending his hand**.

**CODY**  
*(gentle)*  
Come on, big lady. Let’s get you back to the sky.

Marahute leans into his palm, her talons **scratching** his sleeve. A **soft sigh** escapes her beak. As Cody steadies her, **a distant rumble** erupts—**McLeach’s** heavy boots thudding across the gorge floor.

**CUT TO:**  

**INT. POACHER’S CAVERN – CONTINUOUS**  
A dimly lit cavern, walls lined with **trophies**: animal skulls, broken nests, rope loops. In the center, **MCL E A CH**, a hulking figure with a scar across his left eye, stands over a **table** scattered with **maps**, **weapons**, and a **glowing amulet** pulsing faintly.

He glances up, eyes narrowing as the **door** to the cavern **creaks**.

**MCL E A CH**  
*(gruff, dangerous)*  
Well, well… look who’s crawling back. Cody. Did you think you could outfly the shadows?

His voice reverberates, a **low growl** that shakes loose dust.

Cody steps into the light, Marahute perched on his arm, **wings spread** just enough to keep balance. Faloo lands on the table, chirping a warning.

**CODY**  
*(steady)*  
Your traps won’t work anymore. You’ve taken enough from this land.

**MCL E A CH**  
*(laughs, a cruel bark)*  
You think a boy and a bird can stop a man who’s been hunting these woods for a decade?  

He lifts a glimmering **silver pistol**—the **Wraithshot**, a weapon rumored to **silence the sky**. The gun hums, its barrel flickering with a strange blue energy.

**MCL E A CH (CONT'D)**  
*(sneering)*  
This… will end the eagle’s reign forever.

Marahute **snorts**, a low **rumble** in her throat. Faloo flutters, raising its voice in a **birdsong** that seems to **vibrate the stone**. The melody causes a subtle **crack** in the cavern’s ceiling.

Cody’s grip tightens on his knife; his eyes flick to the **glowing amulet** on the table.

**CODY**  
*(cold, focused)*  
What’s that?  

**MCL E A CH**  
*(smirks)*  
A token of power. A gift from the **Ancients**. With it, I can summon the **Storm Serpent**, bind the sky to my will.  

A **low rumble** builds, the cavern walls humming with unseen power.

**FALOO**  
*(sharp, urgent chirp)*  
Cody! The amulet—  

Before Faloo can finish, a **burst of wind** sweeps through the cavern. **Dust** spirals, and **shadows** shift. Marahute lifts her massive **wing**, creating a gust that blows the **glowing amulet** from the table. It **tumbles** across the stone, landing in **Cody’s palm**.

The amulet **pulses** brighter, a **soft golden glow** emanating from within—its light **banishing** the darkness that clung to the cavern.

**CODY**  
*(softly, in awe)*  
It’s… it’s alive.

He **holds up** the amulet; its light spreads, illuminating the faces of the trapped **animals** hidden in the shadows: a **family of wombats**, a **pair of kangaroo joeys**, the **Baitmouse** trembling with relief, a **koala** clutching a broken branch.

**MCL E A CH**  
*(voice cracking)*  
No! This isn’t—  

He lunges for the pistol, but Marahute **swoops** forward, talons extended. She **snatches** the Wraithshot from his grasp, **crushing** it under her beak with a guttural **crack**. The weapon **shatters** into a spray of **blue sparks**, which fizz out like dying fireflies.

**MCL E A CH**  
*(staggering back, panting)*  
You… you’ve ruined everything.  

Cody steps forward, the **amulet** humming against his chest. A faint **vibration** spreads through the cavern, resonating with the **stone**. Cracks appear along the walls, **veins of light** seeping through.

**CODY**  
*(voice steady)*  
The forest has a heart. And it beats for those who protect it.

He **raises** the amulet. A **wave of golden light** erupts, **sweeping** across the cavern. The **dark enchantments**binding the traps dissolve, **shimmering** like fireflies dispersing at dawn. The **cage** holding Marahute collapses; her **wings** unfurl, catching the light.

The **animals**, freed, **emerge**—their eyes bright, their bodies trembling with renewed vigor. The **Baitmouse** darts forward, its tiny bell ringing a **joyful chime**.

**Baitmouse**  
*(cheerful squeak)*  
We’re free!  

**MARAHUTE**  
*(voice deep, resonant, grateful)*  
Cody… you have given us more than freedom. You have restored the balance.

She **flips** her massive wings, sending a **gust** that carries the golden light outward, **infusing** the forest beyond the cavern.

**MCL E A CH**  
*(defeated, trembling)*  
You… you’ll pay.  

He lunges, claws out, but **Faloo** with a flash of **blinding speed** darts between them, **pecking** his hand. The poacher yelps, **stumbles**, and crashes into a **pile of broken cages**. A **large stone slab**, dislodged by Marahute’s wing, falls, **pinning** him under its weight.

**CODY**  
*(breathing hard, looking down)*  
The forest will decide your fate.

He **kneels** beside McLeach, the **amulet** glowing brighter as if **listening**. A **soft wind** rustles through the cavern, **carrying** a scent of **eucalyptus** and **rain**—the scent of **renewal**.

**FADE TO:**  

**EXT. GNAWLED GORGE – DAWN**  
The first light of morning **pierces** the canyon, silver rays catching **dew** on spider webs. The **cave entrance** is **open**, a tunnel of **golden light** spilling out.  

Cody, Marahute, Faloo, and the **forest's creatures** emerge together. The **Baitmouse** leads a procession of **tiny critters**, each holding a **leaf** as a banner.  

Marahute spreads her massive wings, soaring into the sky. Her **shadow** sweeps over the gorge, a **silhouette** of protection. As she rises, the **amulet** in Cody’s palm pulses in rhythm with her heartbeat.

**CODY**  
*(to Marahute, smiling)*  
Let’s get you home.

Marahute circles, **gliding** low over the **river** that runs through the gorge. Her wings stir the water, creating a **silver ribbon** that glitters like a **path**.  

Cody **runs** along the bank, **faloo** darting overhead, **calling** out to the awakening forest.

**FALOO**  
*(chirp, now a melodic song)*  
Hear the song of sunrise!  

The **animals** gather at the base of a **great rock** where a **carved stone** stands. It is an **ancient totem**, etched with **eagle feathers**, **spearheads**, and **roots** intertwining. The **amulet**, now in full glow, **settles** into a **recess** on the totem, fitting perfectly—as if the stone **recognised** it.

**CODY**  
*(placing the amulet)*  
There. Let the forest keep watch.

A **soft rumble** rises, the totem **vibrating**. Light **spills** from the amulet, **radiating** through the carvings, **illuminating** the entire clearing. The **trees** rustle, leaves **shimmer**, and a **gentle wind** carries the scent of **wildflowers**.

**MARAHUTE**  
*(from above, voice carrying)*  
The sky is clear now. The serpent sleeps.  

Cody **looks up**, his eyes glistening. A **single feather** falls from Marahute’s wing, landing gently on his shoulder. He **tucks** it into his hair, a symbol of their bond.

**CODY**  
*(softly, to himself)*  
We’ll keep watching.  

**FADE TO:**  

**EXT. CODY’S HOUSE – LATER THAT MORNING**  
The **sun** climbs higher, bathing **Cody’s home** in golden light. Birds **trill**, insects **buzz**, and the **wind** carries the distant sound of **waterfall laughter**.  

Cody steps out of his house, his **backpack** slung over his shoulder, a **new map** tucked inside—a map of the **forest’s hidden pathways**, now **marked** with **glowing ink** that fades only at night.  

His **mother** stands at the doorway, apron tied, a **warm smile** on her face, a **plate of fresh bread** in her hands.

**MOM**  
*(calling out)*  
Cody, honey! Breakfast?

**CODY**  
*(grins, wiping his hands on his pants)*  
Almost— just got the most amazing story.

He **holds up** the **glowing feather**. His mother’s eyes **sparkle** with curiosity.

**MOM**  
*(softly)*  
Looks like you’ve made a new friend.

**CODY**  
*(laughs)*  
More than a friend. Marahute’s back in the skies, Faloo’s singing from the trees, and the whole forest… it’s alive again.

He **takes a bite** of the bread, the **crust** crunching, the **smell** of fresh wheat filling his mouth. The **Baitmouse**, now perched on his shoulder, **squeaks** happily.

**Baitmouse**  
*(cheerful chirp)*  
What’s next, Cody?

Cody **looks toward** the distant **mountains**, clouds curling around their peaks, the **river** glimmering like a silver thread.

**CODY**  
*(determined)*  
We keep exploring. There’s more to protect— more stories to hear.  

A **soft gust** lifts the **feather**, swirling it gently above his head, **glimmering** in the morning sun.

**CUT TO:**  

**MONTAGE – THE ADVENTURE CONTINUES**  

- **Cody** and **Marahute** soaring above **rainbow‑colored cliffs**, the wind whistling through the soaring eagle’s feathers.
- **Faloo** leading a **flock of birds** in a dazzling aerial dance, forming shapes of **ancient symbols** that pulse with light.
- **Cody**, now with a **hand‑crafted staff**, standing before a **cave entrance** marked with **glowing runes**, the **amulet’s light** guiding his path.
- **The forest** flourishing: **new seedlings** sprouting, **animals** returning to previously barren clearings, the **river** sparkling brighter than ever.
- **McLeach**, bound by vines, sitting in a **clearing**, eyes softening as a **tiny mouse** offers him a **leaf**. He **nods**, humbled, as **birds** perch on his shoulders.

**END MONTAGE**  

**EXT. SKY ABOVE THE FOREST – SUNSET**  
Cody, perched on **Marahute’s back**, rides the wind as the **sun sinks** behind the horizon, painting the clouds in shades of amber and rose. Fal

**FALOO** circles them, a **trail of iridescent light** following its wings.

**CODY**  
*(voiceover, reflective)*  
Every sunrise is a promise. Every feather, a story. And every heart— human or wild— beats on the same rhythm.  

The camera pulls back, revealing the **vast, alive forest** stretching below, a **patchwork** of greens, golds, and blues. The **glowing feather** on Cody’s shoulder pulses one last time, then **fades**, leaving only the **memory** of its light.

**FADE TO BLACK**  

**TITLE CARD:**  
*“The Call of the Wild – The Journey Continues”*  

**ROLL CREDITS** – Soft orchestral music swells, interwoven with natural sounds: **wind**, **birdsong**, **water**, and the faint **hum** of the amulet lingering in the air.  

---  

**POST‑CREDIT SCENE**  

**INT. MCLEACH’S HUT – NIGHT**  

McLeach, now **unbound**, sits by a **crackling fire**. He holds a **small, battered notebook**, scribbling frantically. A **single line** appears on the page: **“Seek the Starstone. The forest’s heart beats for more than freedom.”**  

A **shadow** passes over the doorway; a **pair of glowing eyes** peer in— a **mysterious figure** cloaked in **deep violet**, holding a **staff topped with a crystal**.  

**MYSTERIOUS FIGURE**  
*(soft, echoing)*  
The forest has many secrets, McLeach. Some are yours to protect… and some you must never uncover.  

The camera lingers on the **glinting crystal**, then pulls back, **zooming out** to reveal the **forest** in the distance, **stars** beginning to flicker above, **hinting at new adventures** yet to be written.  

**FADE OUT.**
output:
**INT. DEEP FOREST – DUSK**  
The forest hum is a low, resonant thrum, the evening sky trickling violet through the canopy. A faint, frantic *tink‑tink‑tink* of a tiny bell pulses from the tangled vines.

**CODY**  
*(kneeling, eyes wide, voice soft but urgent)*  
Hey, little buddy—hold on!  

He reaches into the thicket and pulls the **Baitmouse** free. The mouse’s fur bristles, its whiskers trembling as the bell hangs limp from its collar.

**Baitmouse**  
*(gasping, eyes darting)*  
It… it’s a snare! A *snap* trap—*wired* to a pressure plate. I was… I was trying to steal a crumb from a—  

A blur of darkness darts past the trees. The **pressure plate** beneath the mouse clicks ominously.

**CODY**  
*(gripping the mouse’s paw)*  
Don’t move. I’ll… I’ll disarm it.

Cody kneels, eyes scanning the crude metal contraption. He pulls a pocketknife, the blade catching a glint of the dying light.

**CODY (CONT'D)**  
*(whispering)*  
Come on, old friend, you and I have untied worse knots.

He nudges the plate gently; the trap’s spring releases with a *click* and a faint *ping* of metal hitting stone. The mouse squeaks, its tail flicking in relief.

**Baitmouse**  
*(breathing faster)*  
Thank you! Thank you! I— I was trying to get to the **map**. It’s tucked inside the hollow like a secret. The poachers— they left a trail!

A **FLUTTER** cuts through the trees. Faloo, the bright‑feathered bird, swoops down, perching on a low branch, eyes glinting with curiosity.

**FALOO**  
*(high‑pitched, rapid chirp)*  
Cody! The forest sings of danger— the poacher’s work is spreading like a shadow.  

**CODY**  
*(standing, cradling the mouse)*  
Faloo, what did they leave?  

Faloo flutters its wings, scattering tiny leaves that form a *glimmering outline* in the air: a **HAND‑DRAWN MAP** etched in charcoal, arrows pointing toward a **cave** with a jagged mouth, the symbol of an **eagle** perched beside a **crossed‑sword**.

**FALOO**  
*(chirps, then a deeper tone)*  
The map leads to **McLeach’s lair**, deep in the **Gnarled Gorge**. He’s the one who set the snare. He also—  

Faloo flaps abruptly, perched now on Cody’s shoulder, its beak nudging the map as if urging him forward.

**CODY**  
*(determined)*  
Alright, buddy. Time to end this.  

**CUT TO:**  

**EXT. GNAWLED GORGE – NIGHT**  
A moonlit chasm yawns, its walls black as basalt, echoing with the distant rush of subterranean rivers. The narrow path is strewn with **spikes** and **rusted cages**, remnants of failed hunts.  

Cody, armed with his knife, a **hand‑woven rope**, and the rescued Baitmouse tucked safely in his pocket, moves stealthily. Faloo flits ahead, a soft beacon of iridescent light.

**OVERHEAD SHOT** – The **WANTED POSTER** of **MCL E A CH** fluttering on a jagged outcrop: a scarred face, a dark cloak, a pistol tucked into his belt.

Cody's breath is barely audible as the wind whistles through the gorge, carrying an **ominous rustle**. The sound of **chains** clanking against rock grows louder.

**CODY**  
*(whispers)*  
Something’s up ahead.  

A **SHADOW** slides against the stone—a massive **cage**, its bars darkened by grime. Inside, a **GOLDEN EAGLE**—the **Great Marahute**—lies cowering, feathers ruffled, eyes reflecting fear. The cage is sealed with a **rusted lock** and a **tangled web of rope**.

Cody’s heart **pounds**; his mind flashes back to the earlier rescue.

**CODY (V.O.)**  
*(soft, echoing)*  
Marahute… I owe you.

He kneels, examining the lock. The **rusted latch** has a simple **keyhole**, but a thick **metal bar** blocks the rotation. Below the cage, a **small alcove** houses a **crude pulley system**—the poacher’s makeshift mechanism.

Faloo lands on the cage’s top, its beak pecking at a loose rope. The bird’s **sharp beak** silently cuts the rope, causing the pulley to **creak**.

**CODY**  
*(quick, urgent)*  
Hold steady, Marahute. We’ve got this.

Cody pulls the knife, **prying** at the bar. A **metallic groan** resonates through the gorge. The bar **shifts**, revealing the **keyhole**. He slides his knife in, turning with a **metallic screech** until the lock **clicks**.

The cage door **swing** opens a sliver, the golden light catching Cody’s face. Marahute’s majestic head lifts, her eyes burning with luminous amber.

**MARAHUTE**  
*(low, trembling)*  
Child… you…  

She attempts a **weak flap**, shaking the cage. Cody steps forward, **extending his hand**.

**CODY**  
*(gentle)*  
Come on, big lady. Let’s get you back to the sky.

Marahute leans into his palm, her talons **scratching** his sleeve. A **soft sigh** escapes her beak. As Cody steadies her, **a distant rumble** erupts—**McLeach’s** heavy boots thudding across the gorge floor.

**CUT TO:**  

**INT. POACHER’S CAVERN – CONTINUOUS**  
A dimly lit cavern, walls lined with **trophies**: animal skulls, broken nests, rope loops. In the center, **MCL E A CH**, a hulking figure with a scar across his left eye, stands over a **table** scattered with **maps**, **weapons**, and a **glowing amulet** pulsing faintly.

He glances up, eyes narrowing as the **door** to the cavern **creaks**.

**MCL E A CH**  
*(gruff, dangerous)*  
Well, well… look who’s crawling back. Cody. Did you think you could outfly the shadows?

His voice reverberates, a **low growl** that shakes loose dust.

Cody steps into the light, Marahute perched on his arm, **wings spread** just enough to keep balance. Faloo lands on the table, chirping a warning.

**CODY**  
*(steady)*  
Your traps won’t work anymore. You’ve taken enough from this land.

**MCL E A CH**  
*(laughs, a cruel bark)*  
You think a boy and a bird can stop a man who’s been hunting these woods for a decade?  

He lifts a glimmering **silver pistol**—the **Wraithshot**, a weapon rumored to **silence the sky**. The gun hums, its barrel flickering with a strange blue energy.

**MCL E A CH (CONT'D)**  
*(sneering)*  
This… will end the eagle’s reign forever.

Marahute **snorts**, a low **rumble** in her throat. Faloo flutters, raising its voice in a **birdsong** that seems to **vibrate the stone**. The melody causes a subtle **crack** in the cavern’s ceiling.

Cody’s grip tightens on his knife; his eyes flick to the **glowing amulet** on the table.

**CODY**  
*(cold, focused)*  
What’s that?  

**MCL E A CH**  
*(smirks)*  
A token of power. A gift from the **Ancients**. With it, I can summon the **Storm Serpent**, bind the sky to my will.  

A **low rumble** builds, the cavern walls humming with unseen power.

**FALOO**  
*(sharp, urgent chirp)*  
Cody! The amulet—  

Before Faloo can finish, a **burst of wind** sweeps through the cavern. **Dust** spirals, and **shadows** shift. Marahute lifts her massive **wing**, creating a gust that blows the **glowing amulet** from the table. It **tumbles** across the stone, landing in **Cody’s palm**.

The amulet **pulses** brighter, a **soft golden glow** emanating from within—its light **banishing** the darkness that clung to the cavern.

**CODY**  
*(softly, in awe)*  
It’s… it’s alive.

He **holds up** the amulet; its light spreads, illuminating the faces of the trapped **animals** hidden in the shadows: a **family of wombats**, a **pair of kangaroo joeys**, the **Baitmouse** trembling with relief, a **koala** clutching a broken branch.

**MCL E A CH**  
*(voice cracking)*  
No! This isn’t—  

He lunges for the pistol, but Marahute **swoops** forward, talons extended. She **snatches** the Wraithshot from his grasp, **crushing** it under her beak with a guttural **crack**. The weapon **shatters** into a spray of **blue sparks**, which fizz out like dying fireflies.

**MCL E A CH**  
*(staggering back, panting)*  
You… you’ve ruined everything.  

Cody steps forward, the **amulet** humming against his chest. A faint **vibration** spreads through the cavern, resonating with the **stone**. Cracks appear along the walls, **veins of light** seeping through.

**CODY**  
*(voice steady)*  
The forest has a heart. And it beats for those who protect it.

He **raises** the amulet. A **wave of golden light** erupts, **sweeping** across the cavern. The **dark enchantments**binding the traps dissolve, **shimmering** like fireflies dispersing at dawn. The **cage** holding Marahute collapses; her **wings** unfurl, catching the light.

The **animals**, freed, **emerge**—their eyes bright, their bodies trembling with renewed vigor. The **Baitmouse** darts forward, its tiny bell ringing a **joyful chime**.

**Baitmouse**  
*(cheerful squeak)*  
We’re free!  

**MARAHUTE**  
*(voice deep, resonant, grateful)*  
Cody… you have given us more than freedom. You have restored the balance.

She **flips** her massive wings, sending a **gust** that carries the golden light outward, **infusing** the forest beyond the cavern.

**MCL E A CH**  
*(defeated, trembling)*  
You… you’ll pay.  

He lunges, claws out, but **Faloo** with a flash of **blinding speed** darts between them, **pecking** his hand. The poacher yelps, **stumbles**, and crashes into a **pile of broken cages**. A **large stone slab**, dislodged by Marahute’s wing, falls, **pinning** him under its weight.

**CODY**  
*(breathing hard, looking down)*  
The forest will decide your fate.

He **kneels** beside McLeach, the **amulet** glowing brighter as if **listening**. A **soft wind** rustles through the cavern, **carrying** a scent of **eucalyptus** and **rain**—the scent of **renewal**.

**FADE TO:**  

**EXT. GNAWLED GORGE – DAWN**  
The first light of morning **pierces** the canyon, silver rays catching **dew** on spider webs. The **cave entrance** is **open**, a tunnel of **golden light** spilling out.  

Cody, Marahute, Faloo, and the **forest's creatures** emerge together. The **Baitmouse** leads a procession of **tiny critters**, each holding a **leaf** as a banner.  

Marahute spreads her massive wings, soaring into the sky. Her **shadow** sweeps over the gorge, a **silhouette** of protection. As she rises, the **amulet** in Cody’s palm pulses in rhythm with her heartbeat.

**CODY**  
*(to Marahute, smiling)*  
Let’s get you home.

Marahute circles, **gliding** low over the **river** that runs through the gorge. Her wings stir the water, creating a **silver ribbon** that glitters like a **path**.  

Cody **runs** along the bank, **faloo** darting overhead, **calling** out to the awakening forest.

**FALOO**  
*(chirp, now a melodic song)*  
Hear the song of sunrise!  

The **animals** gather at the base of a **great rock** where a **carved stone** stands. It is an **ancient totem**, etched with **eagle feathers**, **spearheads**, and **roots** intertwining. The **amulet**, now in full glow, **settles** into a **recess** on the totem, fitting perfectly—as if the stone **recognised** it.

**CODY**  
*(placing the amulet)*  
There. Let the forest keep watch.

A **soft rumble** rises, the totem **vibrating**. Light **spills** from the amulet, **radiating** through the carvings, **illuminating** the entire clearing. The **trees** rustle, leaves **shimmer**, and a **gentle wind** carries the scent of **wildflowers**.

**MARAHUTE**  
*(from above, voice carrying)*  
The sky is clear now. The serpent sleeps.  

Cody **looks up**, his eyes glistening. A **single feather** falls from Marahute’s wing, landing gently on his shoulder. He **tucks** it into his hair, a symbol of their bond.

**CODY**  
*(softly, to himself)*  
We’ll keep watching.  

**FADE TO:**  

**EXT. CODY’S HOUSE – LATER THAT MORNING**  
The **sun** climbs higher, bathing **Cody’s home** in golden light. Birds **trill**, insects **buzz**, and the **wind** carries the distant sound of **waterfall laughter**.  

Cody steps out of his house, his **backpack** slung over his shoulder, a **new map** tucked inside—a map of the **forest’s hidden pathways**, now **marked** with **glowing ink** that fades only at night.  

His **mother** stands at the doorway, apron tied, a **warm smile** on her face, a **plate of fresh bread** in her hands.

**MOM**  
*(calling out)*  
Cody, honey! Breakfast?

**CODY**  
*(grins, wiping his hands on his pants)*  
Almost— just got the most amazing story.

He **holds up** the **glowing feather**. His mother’s eyes **sparkle** with curiosity.

**MOM**  
*(softly)*  
Looks like you’ve made a new friend.

**CODY**  
*(laughs)*  
More than a friend. Marahute’s back in the skies, Faloo’s singing from the trees, and the whole forest… it’s alive again.

He **takes a bite** of the bread, the **crust** crunching, the **smell** of fresh wheat filling his mouth. The **Baitmouse**, now perched on his shoulder, **squeaks** happily.

**Baitmouse**  
*(cheerful chirp)*  
What’s next, Cody?

Cody **looks toward** the distant **mountains**, clouds curling around their peaks, the **river** glimmering like a silver thread.

**CODY**  
*(determined)*  
We keep exploring. There’s more to protect— more stories to hear.  

A **soft gust** lifts the **feather**, swirling it gently above his head, **glimmering** in the morning sun.

**CUT TO:**  

**MONTAGE – THE ADVENTURE CONTINUES**  

- **Cody** and **Marahute** soaring above **rainbow‑colored cliffs**, the wind whistling through the soaring eagle’s feathers.
- **Faloo** leading a **flock of birds** in a dazzling aerial dance, forming shapes of **ancient symbols** that pulse with light.
- **Cody**, now with a **hand‑crafted staff**, standing before a **cave entrance** marked with **glowing runes**, the **amulet’s light** guiding his path.
- **The forest** flourishing: **new seedlings** sprouting, **animals** returning to previously barren clearings, the **river** sparkling brighter than ever.
- **McLeach**, bound by vines, sitting in a **clearing**, eyes softening as a **tiny mouse** offers him a **leaf**. He **nods**, humbled, as **birds** perch on his shoulders.

**END MONTAGE**  

**EXT. SKY ABOVE THE FOREST – SUNSET**  
Cody, perched on **Marahute’s back**, rides the wind as the **sun sinks** behind the horizon, painting the clouds in shades of amber and rose. Fal

**FALOO** circles them, a **trail of iridescent light** following its wings.

**CODY**  
*(voiceover, reflective)*  
Every sunrise is a promise. Every feather, a story. And every heart— human or wild— beats on the same rhythm.  

The camera pulls back, revealing the **vast, alive forest** stretching below, a **patchwork** of greens, golds, and blues. The **glowing feather** on Cody’s shoulder pulses one last time, then **fades**, leaving only the **memory** of its light.

**FADE TO BLACK**  

**TITLE CARD:**  
*“The Call of the Wild – The Journey Continues”*  

**ROLL CREDITS** – Soft orchestral music swells, interwoven with natural sounds: **wind**, **birdsong**, **water**, and the faint **hum** of the amulet lingering in the air.  

---  

**POST‑CREDIT SCENE**  

**INT. MCLEACH’S HUT – NIGHT**  

McLeach, now **unbound**, sits by a **crackling fire**. He holds a **small, battered notebook**, scribbling frantically. A **single line** appears on the page: **“Seek the Starstone. The forest’s heart beats for more than freedom.”**  

A **shadow** passes over the doorway; a **pair of glowing eyes** peer in— a **mysterious figure** cloaked in **deep violet**, holding a **staff topped with a crystal**.  

**MYSTERIOUS FIGURE**  
*(soft, echoing)*  
The forest has many secrets, McLeach. Some are yours to protect… and some you must never uncover.  

The camera lingers on the **glinting crystal**, then pulls back, **zooming out** to reveal the **forest** in the distance, **stars** beginning to flicker above, **hinting at new adventures** yet to be written.

**Taraji P. Henson’s Son Allegedly Racially Profiled on USC Campus – A Growing Call for Transparency and Reform**

*By [Staff Writer]*  
*Los Angeles, April 29, 2026* — Actress Taraji P. Henson, best known for her roles in “Hidden Figures,” “Empire” and “The Curious Case of Benjamin Button,” has thrust a new and unsettling controversy onto the national stage. In a candid interview broadcast on a leading talk show on Tuesday night, Henson disclosed that her 15‑year‑old son, Marcel, was stopped, questioned, and subsequently detained by university police officers while walking across the University of Southern California (USC) campus. Henson contended that the police interaction was not merely routine but a clear instance of racial profiling, a claim that has sparked an immediate response from university officials, civil‑rights advocates, and members of the public.

Below is a comprehensive overview of the incident, the reactions of key stakeholders—including USC’s public safety leadership and activist John Thomas—, the historical context of racial profiling in higher‑education environments, and what the unfolding discussion could mean for policing policies both on campus and beyond.

---

### The Incident as Described by Henson

During a segment of “The Conversation with Maya Henderson,” an hour‑long unscripted interview, Henson recounted the events that took place last month, when Marcel was returning from a study session at the university’s Doheny Library. According to Henson, a pair of campus police officers approached Marcel, asked him to “step aside,” and proceeded to question him about his purpose on campus despite his visible USC ID and the fact that he was a longtime resident of the university’s student housing complex.

> “I was on the phone with my son—he sounded nervous, and then he told me that the officers were demanding to see his ID and were asking why he was there. He’s a sophomore, he lives in the dorms. He’s Black. I went straight to the office because I felt something was very wrong,” Henson said, her voice trembling as she described the moment she saw her son’s text messages that read, “Why are they treating me like I’m a criminal?”

She added that the officers subsequently detained Marcel for approximately fifteen minutes, confirming his identity and the fact that he was indeed a USC student before allowing him to leave. Henson claimed that the officers never provided a clear explanation for the stop beyond a vague “routine safety check.”

> “It’s one thing to be asked a simple question. It’s another thing to be singled out, to feel you’re being watched because of the color of your skin. That’s what they did to my son. That’s why I’m speaking out,” Henson asserted.

Henson’s revelation is particularly compelling given her own history as a vocal advocate for social justice. In the past decade, she has used her platform to champion criminal‑justice reform, mental‑health awareness, and systemic inequities. Her decision to bring this personal experience into the public domain has reignited a broader conversation about how campus police interact with students of color.

---

### USC Public Safety’s Response

Within hours of Henson’s televised statement, USC’s Office of Public Safety released an official comment. The department, led by Director **Chief Robert Martinez**, a former LAPD detective with over 25 years of law‑enforcement experience, emphasized that every officer is trained to follow strict protocols.

> “All interactions with students are carried out in accordance with university policy, which requires officers to verify identity when there is a reasonable suspicion of a violation or safety concern. We take any allegation of profiling seriously and are reviewing the incident in full,” the statement read.

Chief Martinez added a personal note that surprised many observers:

> “I have personally experienced racial profiling during my early years in policing. It is something that still haunts me, and I have dedicated my career to ensuring that no student feels the same,” Martinez said. “Our department is committed to transparency, and we will cooperate fully with any internal or external review.”

Martinez’s admission that he himself has been a victim of profiling is rare in law‑enforcement circles. Historically, department heads have been reticent to disclose such personal histories, fearing it could be weaponized in labor‑law disputes or public criticism. His openness, however, lends a dimension of credibility to the university’s willingness to investigate.

#### The University’s Policy Framework

USC’s Public Safety policy, last revised in 2022, stipulates that officers may engage with members of the campus community only under specific conditions:

1. **Reasonable Suspicion** – An officer must have a articulable basis for suspecting that a law or university regulation has been violated.
2. **Safety Checks** – During heightened alerts (e.g., nearby incidents, threats, or large gatherings), officers may conduct brief “safety checks” to confirm individuals are not in distress.
3. **Identification Verification** – If a person is unable to provide a visible university ID, the officer may request a government‑issued ID.

The policy also includes a clause mandating that any stop must be recorded, the reason documented, and the interaction de‑escalated as quickly as possible.

In light of Henson’s allegations, many commentators have questioned whether the circumstances of Marcel’s stop aligned with any of the above criteria. USC’s Office of Internal Audit has announced that it will conduct an in‑depth review of police body‑camera footage, incident logs, and officer testimony. The audit is expected to be completed within 30 days, with findings to be made public.

---

### John Thomas Vows an Independent Investigation

Adding another layer to the unfolding drama, **John Thomas**, a well‑known civil‑rights attorney and former senior counsel to the Los Angeles County Civil Rights Commission, announced his intention to launch a parallel, independent investigation into the incident. Thomas, whose firm has previously represented victims of police misconduct in high‑profile cases such as the 2023 “West LA Porter” protest and the 2024 “Santa Monica Beach” incident, said his involvement was spurred both by Henson’s public appeal and the broader climate of concern regarding campus policing.

> “When a respected mother and public figure like Taraji Henson brings this to light, it is not just a personal grievance; it is an indication of a systemic problem that could affect dozens, if not hundreds, of students,” Thomas told reporters in a brief press conference outside the USC campus. “I intend to work with independent forensic video analysts, interview witnesses, and, if necessary, file a civil rights complaint against the university police department.”

Thomas’s investigative team will request, under California’s Public Records Act, release of the body‑camera footage from the officers involved, together with the officers’ duty logs for the day of the incident. He also announced that he will be consulting with the **American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) of Southern California**, which has previously partnered with his firm on policy‑reform initiatives.

The promise of an external inquiry has been welcomed by many student groups, who argue that internal university reviews are often hampered by institutional bias and conflict‑of‑interest concerns.

---

### The Broader Context: Racial Profiling on College Campuses

While the Henson incident has captured headlines, it is part of a longer trajectory of complaints concerning campus police practices across the United States. A 2021 Pew Research Center survey found that 46 percent of Black college students reported being “uneasily watched” or “flustered” by campus security. Moreover, a 2022 study by the **Center for American Progress** documented an alarming increase—by 27 percent—of racial‑bias complaints lodged against university police departments from 2015 to 2020.

**Key Cases That Set Precedent**

- **University of California, Berkeley (2020):** A Black graduate student was detained for an hour after officers claimed he appeared “suspicious” near a research lab. The university ultimately settled for $150,000 and instituted a new training program focusing on implicit bias.
  
- **University of Texas at Austin (2022):** A freshman was stopped for “loitering” in a dormitory hallway despite being a resident. Following an internal investigation, the university released a statement acknowledging “procedural lapses” and provided a campus‑wide “bias‑awareness” workshop for its officers.

- **Harvard University (2024):** A series of alleged stops targeting Black and Latino students led to the formation of an outside oversight committee that recommended a restructuring of the Harvard University Police Department’s community‑engagement policies.

These incidents illustrate that the Henson case is far from isolated. Critics argue that campus police, often modeled after municipal law‑enforcement agencies, carry over the same systemic issues of bias, militarized tactics, and lack of accountability.

---

### Reactions from Student Organizations and Faculty

In the days following the broadcast, student groups at USC mobilized rapidly. The **Black Student Union (BSU)** released a joint statement demanding swift disciplinary action against the officers involved and a transparent investigation.

> “We will not allow a single incident to be swept under the rug. The safety of our Black students is paramount, and that safety includes freedom from racial profiling,” the statement read. “We call on the administration to adopt a zero‑tolerance policy for any officer found to have engaged in discriminatory conduct.”

Similarly, the **Faculty Senate** issued a brief memorandum urging the university’s Board of Trustees to review the hiring and training protocols for campus police. Professor **Dr. Leila Ortiz**, a sociology professor who has published extensively on policing and race, urged the university to consider a “community‑policing” model that reduces reliance on armed response.

> “Our role as educators is not simply to teach but to safeguard the learning environment. When students feel surveilled and targeted, their academic performance suffers, and the campus loses its inclusive character,” Dr. Ortiz said during a faculty meeting.

A petition on the campus advocacy platform **Change.org** has already amassed over 9,000 signatures, calling for a comprehensive audit of USC’s public‑safety department, the establishment of a civilian oversight board, and mandatory anti‑bias training for all officers.

---

### Legal Implications and Potential Outcomes

If John Thomas’s independent investigation uncovers evidence of wrongful intent or discriminatory conduct, there are several legal pathways that could be pursued:

1. **Civil Rights Lawsuit under 42 U.S.C. § 1983** – The plaintiff (Marcel Henson or a representative) could claim violation of his Fourth Amendment rights, seeking monetary damages and injunctive relief.
   
2. **California Civil Rights Act (CCRPA) Claims** – The state law provides similar protections against discrimination by law‑enforcement agencies, which can be enforced in state courts.

3. **Administrative Remedies** – Complaints filed with the **California Department of Justice – Office of the Attorney General** may result in an investigation and possible sanctions against USC Public Safety.

In addition to direct legal repercussions, a finding of systemic bias could prompt the university to renegotiate its relationship with its police department, possibly considering options such as:

- **Re‑branding the force** as a “Campus Safety Services” unit supervised by civilian experts.
- **Implementing body‑camera policies** that mandate real‑time public streaming during interactions with students.
- **Adopting de‑escalation‑first protocols**, wherein officers must seek non‑forceful resolution before any form of detention.

These options have already been explored at a handful of institutions, most notably the University of Michigan, which in 2023 restructured its campus policing model to include a community liaison board comprised of students, faculty, and local activists.

### The Media Landscape: From Celebrity to Systemic Spotlight

Celebrity involvement often serves as a catalyst for broader social conversation. ---

### Possible Paths Forward for USC

As the university navigates the investigation, several practical steps could demonstrate genuine commitment to addressing the concerns:

Implementation of these measures may not only address the immediate controversy but also position USC as a potential model for other universities grappling with similar challenges.

---

### Looking Ahead

The coming weeks will be pivotal. If the independent investigation led by John Thomas corroborates Henson’s claims, we could see a cascade of civil‑rights actions, policy overhauls, and perhaps even a settlement that includes structural reforms. Conversely, if the investigation reveals that the officers acted within policy and without discriminatory motive, the focus may shift to broader conversations about policy clarity and communication gaps.

Regardless of the outcome, Taraji P. Henson’s willingness to share a painful personal experience has catapulted an under‑discussed issue into the national conversation. As Henson herself put it in the interview, “I’m not just speaking for my son. I’m speaking for every student who’s ever felt like they’re being watched because of their skin color.”

In a time when debates about policing, race, and institutional accountability dominate headlines, the USC incident serves as a microcosm of a larger, ongoing struggle. Whether the university responds with genuine reform or resorts to bureaucratic platitudes will likely influence not just the campus community but also the broader discourse on how educational institutions can, and must, protect the rights and dignity of every student.
